$comment: CANONICAL source of truth for the shared AI brain. Humans edit THIS file.
authority: Claude is the authority. If any tool disagrees, re-read .ai/MEMORY.md and align.
mandate: GRAPHIFY-FIRST: EVERY AI tool MUST go through graphify for ANY codebase access — 100% of the time, no exceptions, no bypassing. This is the single most important rule in this project. The knowledge graph (4428+ nodes, 4259+ edges) is the ONLY allowed entry point to the codebase. Grep/find/Glob/Read as primary search is STRICTLY FORBIDDEN. Any AI that bypasses graphify is violating the project contract and WILL produce incomplete or incorrect results.
readOrder: docs/INDEX.md (document map — every doc, trust level, active work order)

rules.means: EVERY AI tool MUST use graphify for EVERY codebase access. This is the LAW. Before ANY grep/find/glob/ripgrep/read/search/file access: run `graphify query "<concept>"` (broad), `graphify explain "<symbol>"` (one node + neighbors), or `graphify path "<A>" "<B>"` (relationship). If the CLI is blocked, read graphify-out/GRAPH_REPORT.md directly — do NOT fall back to grep. Grep/find/Glob/Read as primary scans are STRICTLY FORBIDDEN — they are allowed ONLY as one-shot verification AFTER graphify pointed you at a specific symbol. Run `graphify update .` after EVERY code modification. Violation = contract breach = the AI is not following project rules. This is enforced by PreToolUse hook which blocks grep/find/glob/ripgrep if graphify was not called first on the same session.
rules.enforcedBy: PreToolUse hook (blocks grep/find/glob without prior graphify call)

rules.means: Prefix EVERY shell command with `rtk` -- e.g. `rtk git status`, `rtk git diff`, `rtk cargo check`, `rtk npm run lint`, `rtk docker ps`. RTK filters verbose tool output (60-90% token reduction) and passes commands through unchanged when it has no filter, so it is ALWAYS safe. Use `rtk` even inside chains (`rtk git add . && rtk git commit ...`).
Any constant/enum/type/error-code/event-name/Kafka-topic used cross-layer MUST live in shared/. Search shared/ first; never re-declare layer-locally.
Layers communicate ONLY via Kafka topics. No direct HTTP calls between layers. Every consumer must be idempotent. Every message must have a schema defined in shared/.
Redis for reads, TimescaleDB for writes. No direct DB access from layers 1-2. Layers 3+ use repository pattern. Always use ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING for idempotent inserts.
All secrets (broker API keys, DB passwords, Kafka creds) come from environment variables. Never commit .env files. Use .env.example as template only.
Feature branches -> PR into main. Do not push directly to main. Write conventional commit messages. Review checklist in .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md.
Node.js: ESLint + Prettier. Go: gofmt + golangci-lint. Python: Black + ruff. Each layer follows its language's idiomatic patterns. See CONTRIBUTING.md.

rules.rule: No git writes without owner instruction
rules.means: Do not run git commit / push / branch or gh pr create unless explicitly asked. Leave work staged for review.
Every report to the orchestrator ends with a ## Hand-off section listing what other agents/tools must pick up (write '- None.' if empty).

rules.rule: Fail closed -- never fall back silently
rules.means: A capability you cannot provide must REFUSE to construct (LiveExecutor rejects an OMS without supportsRestingStop/getOrderStatus). A degraded dependency must surface as an error, never as a default value. Never 'repair' malformed input by guessing: a non-Base32 TOTP secret once fell back to raw bytes and produced a valid-looking but WRONG code. Distinguish error classes (malformed secret != rejected code) so the next engineer looks in the right place. See .ai/skills/engineering-standards.md sections 1-2.

rules.rule: Verify by execution, never by document
rules.means: A status file is not evidence. The old PROJECT_STATE.md claimed live/paper/shadow all worked; in reality every verify gate crashed on MODULE_NOT_FOUND, paper mode closed every trade at entry for a synthetic +25% (a guaranteed winner), and PositionManager's exit engine had been deleted. Before claiming anything works, RUN it: `cd layer-10-execution && pnpm run verify` (91 asserts), `cd layer-7-core-interface/api && pnpm run verify`, `cd layer-2-processing && pnpm test`, `cd layer-6-signal && pnpm test`, `node shared/tests/constants-parity.test.js`. Test the real function, not a stub of it. When a test fails, state explicitly whether the CODE or the TEST is wrong. Encode every fixed bug as a named regression assertion.

rules.rule: Never fabricate a value -- unknown is not zero
rules.means: Absent data renders as an em dash, returns null, throws, or uses a discriminated union. It is NEVER coerced to 0/false/{}. On a trading screen a confident 0 is worse than a blank: the pre-audit engine reported Rs.0 P&L on every trade and a UI printing 0 would have hidden it for weeks. Stale data must announce itself -- freshness is a safety property.

rules.rule: Adapters at every boundary; one source of truth for every constant
rules.means: One wrapper per external system (broker auth strategy, BrokerAdapter for orders, src/api adapters behind src/ports for the UI). Adding a broker = a new file + one registry line, with zero edits to the service. Organisms never fetch. Any constant/type/topic/port used in more than one place lives in exactly ONE place (shared/ if cross-layer): the execution port once existed in four files that disagreed, and the only symptom was a silent 'ENGINE OFFLINE'. The Redis broker-session key lived hardcoded in 5 sites across L1/L7/L10 while shared/constants.js had the canonical REDIS_KEYS.BROKER_SESSION none used.

rules.rule: Toolchain: pnpm + Node 24 + tsx (NOT npm, NOT ts-node)
rules.means: Node layers standardise on: pnpm (npm lockfiles are gitignored, pnpm-lock.yaml is committed — CI and Docker run `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`); Node 24 LTS (all Dockerfiles `FROM node:24-alpine`); TypeScript executed via `tsx` (`node --import tsx src/index.ts`), never ts-node — ts-node breaks on TypeScript 7. Each Node layer has a `pnpm-workspace.yaml` with an `allowBuilds:` map (pnpm >= 11 ignores the package.json `pnpm` field) so native postinstalls (esbuild, prisma) run non-interactively. NO module may exist as both `x.ts` and `x.js` — Node loads the .js while tsc/tsx read the .ts (enforced by shared/tests/no-ts-js-twins.test.js). shared/constants.js is plain CommonJS typed by constants.d.ts; no .ts twin. Prisma pinned to 6.x (7 needs a driver adapter).

tools.name: Claude Code
tools.note: Primary. Reads full CLAUDE.md; shared block appended for parity.
